description: Lecture discusses the Tit-for-Tat strategy in the context of the repeated Prisoner's Dilemma, emphasizing cooperation and its justification. The strategy starts with cooperation and mirrors the opponent's previous action, promoting mutual cooperation over time.
topic_summary: Exploration of the Tit-for-Tat strategy in repeated interactions, highlighting its effectiveness in fostering cooperation between players in the Prisoner's Dilemma. Key points include the importance of starting optimistically and the implications of the probability of continuing stages [t=149.7].
